opportunity_title: "Serve and Earn Game Day Crew" Assisting DFW Area Senior Citizens
description_short: Serve with Purpose: Supporting Senior Citizens Through Sporting Events Join our event concessions team and be part of something bigger than game day. This volunteer opportunity supports S.E.…
description_long: Serve with Purpose: Supporting Senior Citizens Through Sporting Events Join our event concessions team and be part of something bigger than game day. This volunteer opportunity supports S.E. Charities, a nonprofit organization dedicated to serving low-income and homebound senior citizens across Southern Dallas. Through your service at concession stands during local events in Frisco, you are directly helping us fund critical programs that provide seniors with hygiene essentials, food support, and basic living necessities. Every item sold at our concession stand contributes to: Weekly meal and grocery support for seniors Hygiene kits for elderly individuals living on fixed incomes Assistance for seniors facing housing instability or financial hardship How Your Service Makes an Impact Your time and effort behind the concession stand helps generate the funding we need to continue showing up for seniors who are often overlooked and underserved. This is more than volunteering; it’s a chance to actively restore dignity, stability, and care to our aging community. Who This Opportunity Is For We welcome individuals who: Need to complete court-ordered or restitution community service hours Are looking to give back in a meaningful and structured way Want to be part of a supportive, team-oriented environment What to Expect Volunteers will assist with: Taking orders and handling cashless transactions Preparing and serving food and beverages Maintaining a clean and organized workspace Providing friendly and respectful customer service No prior experience is required; just a willingness to show up, contribute, and serve with purpose! Why It Matters Many seniors in our community are forced to choose between basic necessities like food, hygiene items, and medication. Your service helps us bridge that gap and remind them they are not forgotten. We believe everyone deserves an opportunity to make a positive impact; no matter their circumstances. Your service here counts! Share this volunteer opportunity!

opportunity_title: Alvarado, TX 76009 - Be a Friend to a Hospice Patient
description_short: Looking for good people to visit with our hospice patients in an assisted living facility. Volunteers will provide companionship and support to not only the patient, but also to the families.…
description_long: Looking for good people to visit with our hospice patients in an assisted living facility. Volunteers will provide companionship and support to not only the patient, but also to the families. Patients receiving hospice care enjoy the friendship of others. Volunteers read to patients, hold their hands, listen to music, sing, watch TV, play games, share stories, or offer caregiver respite. Your companionship ensures that patients have quality at their end of life. Orientation is short and at your own pace. With our flexible schedule, you can work as many hours as you are able/willing to volunteer. Volunteers will complete a Heart to Heart Hospice application, background check, and receive a TB test at no cost to the volunteer. Click Here Share this volunteer opportunity!

opportunity_title: Apply to Become A Part of the KiTNETiC Team! (LEADERSHIP OPPORTUNITY)
description_short: Are you looking for a leadership position? Passionate about creating change in your community? Experienced in social media or outreach management? Then, we're looking for you! KiTNETiC is looking for…
description_long: Are you looking for a leadership position? Passionate about creating change in your community? Experienced in social media or outreach management? Then, we're looking for you! KiTNETiC is looking for ambitious individuals 13+ years old to be a part of our team. REQUIREMENTS AND MORE INFORMATION BELOW! Who is KiTNETiC? KiTNETiC is a youth-led nonprofit that unites volunteers to pack supply and comfort kits for various community causes such as education and healthcare. We host monthly packing sessions and reach out to community partners. To date, we have packed around 19,000 kits across five states. What kind of leadership positions do we have available? Please remember that no position is paid. These positions serve as leadership opportunities for young individuals.* Bolded positions are positions in high demand! Event Planner - Plan packing sessions and outreach events. Kit Scientist Design kits that our organization packs and distributes to communities in need. Expansion Help expand our organization across the nation. Social Media Manager - Manage our social media and photography. Treasurer - Manage our finances and yearly budgets. Graphic Designer - Design social media and event posts. For more information, please visit this document: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1HnrwI8wastPwtf_R4cXvQVDu0XVH2yrrhjwurWTZBI4/edit?usp=sharing REQUIREMENTS! - MUST BE MET Applicants must be based in the DFW area. Applicants must be at least in the eighth grade. Applicants MUST fill out the official application form before August 1st @ 11:59 PM CST: https://forms.gle/wA8AKQLiNWpvZnLU8 Applicants should expect to dedicate at least an hour to KiTNETiC operations each week and attend monthly meetings. For more information, please email kitnetic.us@gmail.com. Share this volunteer opportunity!

description_long:
Take the SoupMobile 'Be Kind' pledge and commit to spreading
kindness in your little corner of the world! This is a wonderful 'VIRTUAL'
volunteer opportunity! Every Friday at 11:00AM CST the SoupMobile will email
you our 'Be Kind' suggestion for the week. Because this is a 'virtual'
volunteer opportunity, it doesn't matter if you live in Dallas, New York, San
Francisco, Japan, Zimbabwe, Germany, Mexico, Canada, Australia or anywhere else
in the world. As long as you have a valid email address, you are good to go!
Your only obligation is to 'Be Kind’ in your corner of the world. May the Lord
bless & keep you. Pastor David Timothy-SoupMobile Church 2423 S. Good Latimer
Expy. Dallas,Texas 75215 Disclaimer: Please understand that because this is a 'virtual' volunteer
opportunity, the SoupMobile is NOT authorized to sign off on volunteer hours.
If you join the SoupMobile 'Be Kind' team, it will have to be because your heart
leads you to do so, not because of the service hours. Share this volunteer opportunity!

description_short:
Your
commitment to the BGCV is a way to ensure the future of our youth by stepping
forward to be there when the community's needs are at their highest level. This
information will hopefully assist…
description_long:
Your
commitment to the BGCV is a way to ensure the future of our youth by stepping
forward to be there when the community's needs are at their highest level. This
information will hopefully assist you in understanding the club’s history and
help identify what is expected from our board members.  We hope your experience with us will be impactful
and create more extraordinary futures. The Governance Committee nominates board members with input
from the CEO. A board member has a
3-year term with the possibility of an extension from both parties. BGCV
engages in comprehensive efforts to ensure that the Club attracts and maintains
a high level of active, diverse and enthusiastic volunteer leadership. The following principles of good practice are provided as a common
perspective on the responsibilities of individual BGCV Board of Directors
members. 1. A director knows the BGCV’s mission, vision, goals, and current
operations and issues. 2. Each director has a fiduciary responsibility to the organization
for sound financial management. 3. A director
actively supports the BGCV’s mission, vision and goals. 4. A director prioritizes
the BGCV in their annual charitable giving and contributes time and expertise
to the Club’s resource development program. 5. A director participates actively on at least one committee of the
Board.  Committee assignments will be
determined in individual, annual conversations with the chair of the Governance
Committee, considering each Board member’s interests/capabilities and the needs
of the BGCV.  Current committees include: a. Executive b. Governance &
Nominating c. Audit d. Finance e. Development f. Facility &
Safety 6. A director
attends meetings well-prepared and participates fully in all matters.  Directors are expected to: a. Attend 8 out of
10 Board meetings per year b. Participate in
regular committee meetings (frequency varies by committee) c. Attend the annual
Board Retreat (typically a Friday in August) d. Participate
actively in and/or contribute to all major fundraising events during the year e. Participate in
other meetings as required (such as sub-committee meetings for fundraising
events) f. Complete the
annual background check and all required safety training as mandated by BGCV
and Boys & Girls Clubs of America. Share this volunteer opportunity!

opportunity_title: Bring the Joy of Art to Seniors in Collin County
description_short: What you’ll do: As an Art Angel, you will lead one‑hour, group‑based therapeutic art experiences in senior communities, using beautiful digital images of artwork to spark stories, memories, and…
description_long: What you’ll do: As an Art Angel, you will lead one‑hour, group‑based therapeutic art experiences in senior communities, using beautiful digital images of artwork to spark stories, memories, and connection. You will guide gentle conversation, ask open‑ended questions, and create a warm, welcoming space where seniors feel seen, heard, and uplifted. ​ This is not “art therapy” or a clinical role; it is a relationship‑centered, conversation‑driven experience that celebrates life through art. ​ Why it matters: Many seniors in our community live with isolation, memory loss, or serious illness — yet they still crave beauty, meaning, and connection. By sharing art with them, you help reduce loneliness, stimulate memories, and restore a sense of dignity and joy. ​ Every visit becomes a chance to say, “You matter. Your stories matter.” Where you’ll serve: This opportunity is for Collin County. You will be matched with senior communities (including memory‑care and assisted‑living settings) within approximately 10 miles of your home. We work to keep your service close to where you live so your volunteering is sustainable and fits your life. ​ When you serve: Weekdays, typically between 10:00 a.m. and 4:00 p.m. ​ Each visit lasts about one hour, plus brief setup and wrap‑up. ​ Hard commitment: serve for at least 6 months. ​ Expected rhythm: 1–2 Art Experiences per month (up to 4 maximum). ​ How it works: Complete our online application ( found here ) and provide: One 1‑page written reference (non‑family). To be provided after initial screening call Complete a background check through VeriFyi and a brief screening call. Attend an orientation (in person or virtual) to learn about our mission, seniors, and the Art Angel role. Shadow at least 6 Art Experiences with experienced Angels over about 2 months, then begin leading your own sessions with ongoing support. To help with costs, an optional reimbursement of $50 per visit is available, with new Angels limited to 4 presentations per month; the heart of the role is service, not payment. ​ Who can do it?: We’re seeking people who are both heart‑led and comfortable in front of a group: Retired/Active educators (teachers, professors, school counselors): Use your rich experience guiding classrooms to inspire seniors through art and story. Retired/Active nurses, social workers, therapists, and healthcare professionals: Bring your deep empathy and communication skills into a setting focused on connection, not clinical care. Future educators and helping‑profession students (education, psychology, social work, medical humanities, art, communications, marketing, business): Gain real‑world experience leading groups, communicating with diverse audiences, and building confidence in public speaking. Anyone with a flexible weekday schedule, strong public‑speaking skills, and a genuine love for seniors. ​ Minimum requirements: Live in Collin County or nearby, willing to serve within 10 miles of your home. Fluent in English and comfortable speaking to small groups. Tech‑savvy Can bring your own laptop Capable of connecting to Wi‑Fi, TVs, or projectors, and troubleshooting basic issues. Reliable transportation and a valid driver’s license. In good health, mindful that many residents are immunocompromised. Able to handle gentle, honest conversations around memory loss and end‑of‑life with compassion and maturity. ​ Boundaries: You will not provide medical or personal‑care tasks and will not be left alone to care for residents; your role is to present, connect, and celebrate through art. Share this volunteer opportunity!
what_to_bring: Join For Love & Art as an Art Angel and bring the therapeutic power of art to seniors in Collin County. You’ll lead one‑hour, group‑based art experiences in senior communities, using beautiful artwork to spark conversations, memories, and joy.

To help with costs, an optional reimbursement of $50 per visit is available, with new Angels limited to 4 presentations per month; the heart of the role is service, not payment. ​ Who can do it?: We’re seeking people who are both heart‑led and comfortable in front of a group: Retired/Active educators (teachers, professors, school counselors): Use your rich experience guiding classrooms to inspire seniors through art and story. Retired/Active nurses, social workers, therapists, and healthcare professionals: Bring your deep empathy and communication skills into a setting focused on connection, not clinical care. Future educators and helping‑profession students (education, psychology, social work, medical humanities, art, communications, marketing, business): Gain real‑world experience leading groups, communicating with diverse audiences, and building confidence in public speaking. Anyone with a flexible weekday schedule, strong public‑speaking skills, and a genuine love for seniors. ​ Minimum requirements: Live in Tarrant County or nearby, willing to serve within 10 miles of your home. Fluent in English and comfortable speaking to small groups. Tech‑savvy Can bring your own laptop Capable of connecting to Wi‑Fi, TVs, or projectors, and troubleshooting basic issues. Reliable transportation and a valid driver’s license. In good health, mindful that many residents are immunocompromised. Able to handle gentle, honest conversations around memory loss and end‑of‑life with compassion and maturity. ​ Boundaries: You will not provide medical or personal‑care tasks and will not be left alone to care for residents; your role is to present, connect, and celebrate through art. Share this volunteer opportunity!
what_to_bring: Join For Love & Art as an Art Angel and bring the therapeutic power of art to seniors in Tarrant County. You’ll lead one‑hour, group‑based art experiences in senior communities, using beautiful artwork to spark conversations, memories, and joy.

description_long: A Mindful Resource Inc. is looking for volunteers to support our Care Before Crisis™ Mental Health & Wellness Fair on Saturday, May 23 from 11:00 AM to 3:00 PM at West Dallas Multipurpose Center. This event will bring together mental health professionals, community organizations, wellness providers, speakers, youth activities, food, music, breakout sessions, and culturally aligned resources for the West Dallas community. A Mindful Resource Inc. is a trust-first, prevention-based nonprofit mental wellness organization serving Black and Brown communities in Dallas. Our mission is to create safe, welcoming pathways to mental health education, support, and care before people reach crisis. Volunteer support is needed for: Event setup and breakdown Guest check-in and registration Greeter and wayfinding support Resource table assistance Care Connections support Workshop and breakout room support Youth activity area support Vendor and speaker assistance General event support Volunteers should be dependable, welcoming, community-minded, and comfortable engaging with people from diverse backgrounds. You do not need to be a mental health professional to volunteer. We welcome students, professionals, community members, service groups, and anyone passionate about helping people access support. Why this matters: Many people in our communities delay care because of stigma, mistrust, lack of access, or not knowing where to start. This event is designed to make mental wellness feel approachable, culturally aligned, and community-centered. By volunteering, you are helping us build the front door to mental health in Dallas. Event Details: Date: Saturday, May 23 Time: 11:00 AM to 3:00 PM Location: West Dallas Multipurpose Center Address: 2828 Fish Trap Rd, Dallas, TX 75212 Volunteer shifts may include setup before the event, support during the event, or breakdown after the event. Contact: A Mindful Resource Inc. Email: volunteer@amindfulresource.org Phone: (214) 506-0691 Website: https://www.amindfulresource.org Instagram: @amindfulresource Care Before Crisis™ Not therapy first. Trust first. description_short: Thank you for your interest in volunteering with The Carolyn's Way Program, a program of 804 Incorporated. Our mission is to remove transportation barriers for low-income individuals by providing…
description_long: Thank you for your interest in volunteering with The Carolyn's Way Program, a program of 804 Incorporated. Our mission is to remove transportation barriers for low-income individuals by providing income-based transportation to and from work. By helping people maintain reliable employment, our volunteers play a direct role in promoting economic stability, self-sufficiency, and stronger communities. As a volunteer, you may assist with transporting clients to and from work, administrative support, outreach efforts, community events, and other activities that help us expand our impact. We value volunteers who are dependable, compassionate, professional, and committed to serving others with dignity and respect. Because many of our volunteers interact directly with vulnerable individuals and may provide transportation services, safety is a top priority. All volunteers may be subject to screening and verification procedures before being approved for service. We also partner with Above and Beyond Tree service occasionally. Individuals may be disqualified from volunteer service if they have any of the following: • Suspended, revoked, or invalid driver's license • No current automobile insurance (for driving-related volunteer roles) • History of DUI, DWI, reckless driving, or serious traffic offenses • Violent criminal history that could pose a safety risk to clients, volunteers, or staff • Falsification of information provided during the application process Additional requirements may apply depending on the volunteer role. We appreciate your willingness to serve and make a difference. Together, we can help ensure that transportation is never the reason someone loses the opportunity to work, support their family, and build a brighter future. Share this volunteer opportunity!

description_short: Addison Cat Crew is seeking dependable volunteers to help care for a managed community cat colony in the Addison area. We have created a very organized and caring system for these cats over the years…
description_long: Addison Cat Crew is seeking dependable volunteers to help care for a managed community cat colony in the Addison area. We have created a very organized and caring system for these cats over the years and they rely on us daily for food, water and love. It is an incredibly rewarding experience to see these cats looking at you with anticipation while some run to greet you. They may live outdoors without the comfort of an indoor home but we do everything we can to make them as safe and comfortable as we can. They have dedicated feeding stations with dishes that are cleaned daily and shelters that are filled with insulating materials and winterized with fresh materials every year. Responsibilities Feed cats at assigned feeding locations (5 stations with 2-3 blocks of each other) Provide fresh water Provide feedback after each feeding to report any unusual activity and fun pictures of the cats :) Follow established feeding guidelines Time Commitment Approximately 1 hour per feeding visit Typically one assigned day every other week Feeding is generally done late afternoon but is flexible to work with volunteers' schedules By volunteering with Addison Cat Crew, you will be joining a group of caring individuals who bond over their love and concern for these cats. We have a Facebook group where we post after each feeding and post pictures of the cats for everyone to enjoy and sometimes have social events. Share this volunteer opportunity!

opportunity_title: Experience in social services? Be a Virtual Mentor for former foster youth!
description_short:
Mentors play a crucial role in supporting college and trade
school students who have aged out of the foster care system. Many of these
students face challenges such as instability, limited family…
description_long:
Mentors play a crucial role in supporting college and trade
school students who have aged out of the foster care system. Many of these
students face challenges such as instability, limited family support, and gaps
in life-skills training. As mentors, individuals serve as trusted adults who
provide encouragement, guidance, and practical assistance, helping students
navigate the complexities of college life and early adulthood. The primary aim of mentoring is not to resolve all issues,
but rather to empower students, foster their confidence, and assist them in
developing the skills and networks necessary for long-term success. Build a Supportive
Relationship Meet
     regularly with your mentee, biweekly and for any special programing from
     ISF (quarterly). Create
     a safe and non-judgmental environment for open conversation. Utilize
     active listening and offer encouragement. Establish
     healthy boundaries while developing a trusting relationship. Provide Practical
Life Guidance Mentors are not expected to be experts in all areas, but should be resourceful and willing to connect students with available resources. ISF offers support and resources to mentors as needed. Support
     mentees in budgeting and financial literacy. Assist
     with time management and goal setting. Help
     mentees navigate housing options. Guide
     students through internship and career exploration. Promote
     professional communication skills. Facilitate
     networking opportunities and career connections. Offer
     advice on graduate school or career pathways. Support Student
Growth Help
     mentees identify their strengths and build self-confidence. Encourage
     independence and support decision-making. Provide
     guidance during challenging times, such as exams, holidays, or housing
     transitions. Encourage spiritual formation and emotional
health Engage in opportunities and discussions on
leadership development Maintain Program
Partnership Participate
     in mentor orientation or training sessions. Communicate
     with program staff if concerns arise. Refer
     students to program resources or professional services when necessary. Attend
     the annual leadership retreat (optional). Attend
     the annual mission trip if applicable. Mentor Qualities Successful mentors demonstrate the following qualities: Empathy
     and trauma-informed awareness Reliability
     and consistency Respect
     for diverse backgrounds and experiences Strong
     listening and communication skills Encouragement
     of student autonomy and growth Vibrant commitment to personal and spiritual
     growth Mentors recognize that trust is established through
patience, consistency, and respect. Preferred
Backgrounds (Not Required) Mentors may come from a variety of professional or personal
backgrounds, including: Professionals
     with career experience or networks Individuals
     who were first-generation college students Adults
     with experience in education, counseling, or youth development Individuals
     with lived experience overcoming adversity or foster care The most important qualification is a genuine commitment to
supporting a student’s success. Share this volunteer opportunity!

description_short: Feed The City – A Community Meal-Packing Event by Tango Charities Join us for Feed The City , a hands-on volunteer experience where individuals, families, and groups come together to make a…
description_long: Feed The City – A Community Meal-Packing Event by Tango Charities Join us for Feed The City , a hands-on volunteer experience where individuals, families, and groups come together to make a difference; one meal at a time. In just two hours, we’ll assemble an incredible amount of meals for those experiencing food insecurity in our community. 🧡 What to Expect At this event, you’ll gather around tables with other volunteers to make sandwiches, pack lunch sacks, and spread some kindness. It’s simple, impactful, and fun for all ages! We encourage you to wear orange to show your support (optional), tie your hair back, and wear bleach-safe clothing. ✅ Key Details Before Registering All participants ages 18+ must register individually below. Youth under 18 must be accompanied by an adult , with a required 5:1 youth-to-adult ratio . Use the calendar below to find and register for upcoming event dates. We ask everyone attending (any age) to bring enough supplies to make 25–30 meals . See the list below for item details. 🛒 What to Bring Please bring all items listed below to make 25–30 complete meal kits. If you’re attending with a group, each person should bring their own full set of items, we do not recommend splitting the list among group members. When shopping, refer to the serving size on the product’s nutrition label to help estimate quantities. Each person should bring: Sliced Bread – Preferably wheat or whole grain for healthier meals Deli Meat – Pre-packaged turkey, chicken, or ham (organic and preservative-free preferred) – No deli counter meat allowed Sliced Cheese – Pre-packaged, real sliced cheese (not processed cheese products) Yellow Mustard – Only standard yellow mustard (no spicy mustard or mayo) Tangerines – Halos, Cuties, or other easy-to-peel varieties Chips – Large, sharable bags made with healthier ingredients and oils – Avoid individual snack bags Sandwich Bags – Zip-top bags only (no fold-over style) 🕘 Event Schedule Arrival Time: 8:30 AM – 9:00 AM Event Begins: 9:00 AM Duration: Approximately 2 hours 🥪 Can't Attend in Person? You can still support the cause by dropping off completed lunch sacks containing: One bottle of water One granola bar Two tangerines OR one apple (in a zip-top bag) One tuna pouch OR applesauce cup A hand-written positive note or drawing on the outside of the bag Unpacked food donations are also welcomed at the event site. 💬 FAQs Do I need to register? Yes. All participants 18+ must register. RSVP on Facebook is also encouraged. Can I bring friends or a group? Absolutely! Just ensure each person brings a full set of supplies. Is there an age limit? No! Feed The City is for all ages. Kids must be supervised. Can I leave early? Yes. You're welcome to stay for as long as you're able. Can I donate money instead of items? We prefer product donations so we can assemble meals on-site. Join us in creating meals and moments that matter. Together, we can fight hunger and feed hope in our communities. Share this volunteer opportunity!

opportunity_title: Foster for the SPCA of Texas
description_short: Please only sign up for one shift. Our Volunteer Team will then contact you to help get you officially scheduled. Please do not come to the shelter until you have been contacted by the foster team.…
description_long: Please only sign up for one shift. Our Volunteer Team will then contact you to help get you officially scheduled. Please do not come to the shelter until you have been contacted by the foster team. The SPCA of Texas is searching for Foster Heroes who are ready to open their hearts and homes to animals in need. By becoming a Foster Hero, you'll provide one animal with a nurturing, loving environment while another takes their place at the shelter. We offer a variety of foster opportunities to fit any lifestyle, and we'll provide everything you need—all you need to bring is love! The more animals fostered, the more lives saved! Visit www.spca.org/foster to fill out an application, or www.spca.org/fosterme to see animals in need. We offer three types of foster programs: Adoption Ambassador Open your home to adoptable animals and help them find forever homes. Send us great photos and bios for online profiles. Promote animals online, around town, and at adoption events. Communicate with potential adopters to find the perfect match. Average foster time: 2-3 weeks. Benefits include better matches, more space in the shelter, and a rewarding experience for all involved. Trust Building & Socialization Foster Provide a safe, loving environment for animals with difficult pasts to regain trust in people. Typically animals from hoarding, neglect, cruelty cases, or those needing obedience work. Benefits include offering a second chance to animals, insight into their true behavior, and preparing them for adoption. Healing & Comfort Foster Provide loving care and attention for animals needing extra support. Includes mother and litter, kittens/puppies, orphaned babies, animals recovering from sickness or surgery, and fospice care for elderly or terminal animals. Benefits include ensuring comfort during recovery, insight into the animal's personality, and a rewarding experience for foster parents. Share this volunteer opportunity!

description_short: My name is Jazz Sharnese, and I am the Founder and President of OpenHeart a faith-based outreach organization serving families throughout the East Dallas 75210 community through monthly free food…
description_long: My name is Jazz Sharnese, and I am the Founder and President of OpenHeart a faith-based outreach organization serving families throughout the East Dallas 75210 community through monthly free food distributions and neighborhood support initiatives centered around “Bearing one another’s Burdens” from Galatians 6:2. For the past several months, in light of the serveral disabilities that I have, OpenHeart has conducted large-scale monthly food distributions every Second Monday of the month from 2:00 p.m. to 4:00 p.m. serving over 400 households at each distribution and helping to sustain approximately 1,000–1,00 individuals including seniors, children, veterans, the disabled, our homeless neighbors and working families living in a food desert community. After 7 months of Soft openings, a couple of weeks ago on May 11th 2026, we celebrated our official Grand Opening which was featured on WFAA Channel 8 News and supported by numerous volunteers, City officials, Churches, organizations, vendors and community partners. As we prepare for the summer heat, we are currently asking the community for donations of: ⛺ Pop-up tents / canopy tents 🛒 Push carts / rolling carts 🛍 Grocery bags or reusable shopping bags 💧 Bottled water/Gatorade for volunteers and families 🧊 Cooling fans or outdoor misting systems and Six feet folding tables and chairs. Our volunteers serve outdoors for many hours each month, and these items would greatly help us continue serving families safely, efficiently, and with dignity during the summer months. We also welcome volunteers and community partners who would like to support future distributions. Please see the attached photos and videos (WFFA Channel 8 News highligt of Grand Opening) from several of our past distributions and our recent Grand Opening that was on May 11th 2026 so you can see the work being done in the community. Thank you for helping us continue to serve others with compassion, dignity, and love. Heart opened, Burdens shared! description_short: Helen's Project is looking for warm, dependable community members to serve as Granny Nannies — temporary, informal childcare supporters who help remove barriers for our clients when life gets in the…
description_long: Helen's Project is looking for warm, dependable community members to serve as Granny Nannies — temporary, informal childcare supporters who help remove barriers for our clients when life gets in the way. Sometimes a parent just needs a nap. Sometimes they have a job interview, a therapy session, or a doctor's appointment — and no one to watch the kids. That's where you come in. What Granny Nannies Do: Offer short-term, temporary babysitting support to Helen's Project clients Assist on an as-needed, flexible basis Help create space for clients to rest, heal, and access opportunity Who We're Looking For: Caring, patient, and dependable individuals Comfortable with children of varying ages Background check may be required Must be local to the client they are supporting Please Note: Hours will not be approved without service confirmation provided to the Helen's Project team. Questions? Email us at care@helensproject.org Share this volunteer opportunity!

description_long: Helen's Project is seeking experienced or aspiring grant writers to help us secure the funding needed to expand affordable, culturally responsive mental health care across the Cotton Belt region of the American South. Every grant secured means more families get access to care. Your words have the power to make that happen. What Grant Writers Will Do: Research and identify grant opportunities aligned with Helen's Project's mission Draft, edit, and submit grant proposals and letters of inquiry Support reporting requirements for awarded grants Collaborate with the Helen's Project leadership team to articulate program needs and impact Who We're Looking For: Experience in grant writing, nonprofit fundraising, or professional writing (preferred) Strong research and communication skills Ability to work independently and meet deadlines Passion for mental health equity, social justice, or community health Please Note: Hours will not be approved without work product submitted and provided to the Helen's Project team. Questions or to submit work? Email us at connect@helensproject.org Type: Virtual Volunteering Share this volunteer opportunity!

opportunity_title: Hugs Cafe- Job Coach for adults with special needs
description_short: The mission of Hugs Café, Inc. is to enhance the lives of adults with intellectual and developmental disabilities through training and employment. Many call our cafe one of the happiest places on…
description_long: The mission of Hugs Café, Inc. is to enhance the lives of adults with intellectual and developmental disabilities through training and employment. Many call our cafe one of the happiest places on earth! As a volunteer job coach, your role is to support, prompt and encourage the Hugs employees in their various roles at the café: greeters, sandwich line, servers, busing tables and at the register. A volunteer job coach will model the task and behavior that the employee is assigned. Volunteers must be at least 14 years old and, if they are below 18, they need to have a parent or guardian volunteer with them. The ideal volunteer can volunteer once a week at the same day and time for at least 4 weeks in a row (as consistency truly helps our employees thrive!). • All regular job coaches in the cafe must complete a background check prior to volunteering with Hugs • Volunteers should wear a Hugs T-shirt, durable pants in kitchen roles or knee length shorts, and closed-toe, non-slip comfortable shoes. • Job coaches should not perform duties without an employee present • Interaction- 1:1 task coach, prompting, encouraging, helping them in different ways depending on their needs (one employee may need one on one hand over hand prompting while another employee doesn’t have as many needs) • The cafe is open 8 am - 3pm Monday-Saturday. The breakfast and lunch menu are served all day. The staff members you are coaching will have a 15-minute or 30-minute break depending on whether they work for over 5 hours. Please cover the position you are coaching or slide to another area to support per direction of the cafe manager. • Volunteers are asked to put their phones away and not take pictures of employees without permission • Please allow the employees to self-correct by asking questions and giving gentle prompts Please fill out a Hugs volunteer application at hugscafe.org/volunteer Share this volunteer opportunity!

description_long: Come join us in serving the community! description_short:
POSITION SUMMARY: Board Members serve as
fiduciaries and strategic leaders of the organization, ensuring it fulfills its
mission, maintains financial integrity, and operates in compliance with…
description_long:
POSITION SUMMARY: Board Members serve as
fiduciaries and strategic leaders of the organization, ensuring it fulfills its
mission, maintains financial integrity, and operates in compliance with legal
and ethical standards. The ideal candidate will be passionate about making
a positive impact in the lives of at risk male youth and possess
strong leadership skills.  Time
commitment: 4-6 hours per month (excludes committee assignments). RESPONSIBILIITES: · Govern the organization in accordance with its
bylaws, and applicable laws and regulations. · Ensure compliance with legal, ethical, and
governance standards. · Be knowledgeable about Mentoring Brother 2
Brother’s mission, vision, programs, and events. · Serve as a leading ambassador of Mentoring
Brother 2 Brother’s mission and programs. · Agree to a one-year board term with the option
of renewal for consecutive terms. · Regularly attend and prepare for quarterly Board
meetings and committee meetings. · Commit to professional development (one or two
trainings are recommended per year). · Volunteer for and willingly accept assignments
and complete them thoroughly and on time. · Serve on at least one committee and attend
committee meetings. · Make a serious commitment to participate
actively in Mentoring Brother 2 Brother work. · Prepare and approve necessary organizational
policies. · Commit to an annual philanthropic commitment as
determined by the Board. · Actively participate in fundraising for the
organization. · Participate in annual self-evaluation of the
Board and personal board service. · Participate in hiring and oversight process of
the President and Vice President. · Stay informed about Board matters, prepare for
meetings, and engage in issues of importance. · Participate in creation of strategic plan and
conduct annual review of the strategic plan. · Get to know other Board members and Mentoring
Brother 2 Brother stakeholders. · Respond to requests for feedback or decisions
between meetings in a timely manner. · Understand financial statements or commit to
learning about nonprofit financial oversight. · Maintain confidentiality and disclose potential
conflicts of interest. QUALIFICATIONS: · Interest in and willingness to support Mentoring
Brother 2 Brother’s goals and objectives · Boldness for building relationships and a
passion to serve the community · Embodies Mentoring Brother 2 Brother’s five core
values · Practices servant leadership · Sound decision-making ability · Demonstrates a strong work ethic · Results-driven and dependable · Highly effective in working with others · Commitment to open and honest communication · Corporate networking, grant writing,
fundraising, strategic planning or lived experiences · Ability to easily use email and other technology
such as cloud services · Previous board service, a plus Click Here Click Here Share this volunteer opportunity!

description_short: Volunteer Opportunity: Gardening, Composting & Farm Finance Join us for a rewarding volunteer experience focused on gardening, composting, and agricultural education. Volunteers will have the…
description_long: Volunteer Opportunity: Gardening, Composting & Farm Finance Join us for a rewarding volunteer experience focused on gardening, composting, and agricultural education. Volunteers will have the opportunity to learn hands-on skills such as planting, harvesting, compost management, soil improvement, and basic garden maintenance while helping support a community-based growing project. Participants will also be introduced to the financial side of farming, including budgeting, planning, recordkeeping, marketing, and understanding how farms and agricultural projects generate income. This unique opportunity combines practical agricultural knowledge with valuable financial literacy skills. No prior experience is required. Whether you are interested in gardening, sustainability, homesteading, farming, or simply learning something new, we welcome volunteers of all ages and skill levels. Training and guidance will be provided throughout the program. Our goal is to promote environmental stewardship, food production, self-sufficiency, and financial awareness while building a stronger and more connected community. Volunteers will gain valuable experience, meet like-minded individuals, and contribute to a project that supports education, sustainability, and personal growth. Come grow with us and discover how healthy soil, responsible stewardship, and sound financial practices work together to create successful agricultural opportunities. Share this volunteer opportunity!

description_short: Please only sign up for 1 shift. Our Volunteer Team will then contact you to help get you officially scheduled. Please do not come to the shelter until you have been contacted & scheduled. Volunteers…
description_long: Please only sign up for 1 shift. Our Volunteer Team will then contact you to help get you officially scheduled. Please do not come to the shelter until you have been contacted & scheduled. Volunteers are crucial to our shelter's operations, especially for behind-the-scenes tasks. This includes dishwashing, laundry assistance, and cleaning duties, all of which are essential for maintaining a safe and sanitary environment for our furry residents. Keeping our shelter clean directly impacts the health and well-being of our animals. The cleaner the environment, the healthier they can stay. We offer flexible shifts every day of the week, from 9 am to 6 pm. Anyone aged 10 and above is welcome to volunteer. For volunteers under 15, participation alongside a parent or guardian, aged 18 or older, is required. For younger volunteers, aged 10 to 11, opportunities are primarily focused on cat handling after training, while those aged 12 and older can handle both dogs and cats. To get started, you'll need to create a volunteer account and complete a few initial steps. There's a $15 volunteer fee per person to cover the initial costs of onboarding new volunteers. Once these steps are completed, you'll be eligible to sign up for an Orientation session, where you'll receive further training and information. The process to become a volunteer is as follows: Express your interest through Voly. You will then be contacted with a link to complete a SPCA of Texas volunteer application. Upon completing the application, you will need to fulfill a few initial steps. After your initial steps are completed, you should have access through your volunteer account to see and sign up for Orientations. Please refrain from going to the shelter until your scheduled Orientation date. Share this volunteer opportunity!

description_long:
ACH's Mentoring Initiative is a network of supportive
volunteers that helps young people aging out of foster care by providing a
sense of belonging and connection. Volunteer mentors build relationships by
participating in group events and activities with teens and young adults at ACH
Child and Family Services. If you are: easy-going and friendly, patient and
reliable, a good listener and willing to get out of your comfort zone, you can
make a difference for young people aging out of foster care! Mentoring groups meet twice per month on alternating
Tuesdays from 7-9 pm in Fort Worth. Volunteers must be at least 25 years old,
able to pass a full background check and drug screen, and commit for a minimum
of one year. Volunteer Mentors at ACH: • Are warm and accepting, natural "connectors" willing to get out of their comfort zone • Spend 4-6 hours a month with youth and young adults aging
out of foster care. • Commit to being part of a diverse group of caring,
committed, and open-minded volunteers for a minimum of one year. • Participate in group events to help develop natural
connections with mentees. • Engage in intensive training and support. Volunteers must: • Be 25 years or older. • Complete application, background check & drug screen. • Commit to at least one year. · Be open to working with and affirming all young
people, regardless of race, disability, gender identity and sexual orientation. • Participate in
required trainings prior to and during the program. Minimum 1 year commitment, 4-6 hours per month (every other
Tuesday evening from 7-9 pm plus occasional weekend afternoons). Share this volunteer opportunity!

opportunity_title: Mobile Food Pantry: Our Lady of Perpetual Help
description_short: Our mobile community pantries and refrigerated box trucks travel through the nine counties of the Diocese of Dallas to bring fresh food to those in need. Provide courteous and excellent service to…
description_long: Our mobile community pantries and refrigerated box trucks travel through the nine counties of the Diocese of Dallas to bring fresh food to those in need. Provide courteous and excellent service to CCD clients while assisting the OLPH pantry Please Signup Directly on our Website: LINK By choosing to spend your time in the food pantry, you represent the face of hope to families and individuals who are turning to us for help. Duties/Responsibilities: Checking in clients using iPads Bagging or boxing food Placing food in client vehicle trunks Replenishing the stock of food items Assisting with directing traffic What makes a successful volunteer: Able to adhere to safety regulations set in place by the agency Able to use hands to handle food items and lift medium-sized boxes (between 16 – 20 pounds) Ability to stand for hours at a time Helpful & courteous Dependable & reliable Dress code : Closed-toe shoes are required (no sandals or flip-flops). We recommend dressing in comfortable and casual clothes suitable for the season. We recommend sunscreen, sunglasses, work gloves, and hats. Age Requirements: 14 is the minimum age. Volunteers 14-17 must be accompanied by a parent or guardian at all times & must submit their Minor Volunteer Waiver before the shift. Commitment: Our hungry neighbors are counting on you to arrive on time and to stay for the entire shift. If you are unable to complete the full shift, then please log into your account and cancel your registration. We hope you can join us another time. We appreciate your dedication to serving our community! Share this volunteer opportunity!

opportunity_title: Mom's with time to serve students. Be a Virtual Mentor!
description_short:
Mentors play a crucial role in supporting college and trade
school students who have aged out of the foster care system. Many of these
students face challenges such as instability, limited family…
description_long:
Mentors play a crucial role in supporting college and trade
school students who have aged out of the foster care system. Many of these
students face challenges such as instability, limited family support, and gaps
in life-skills training. As mentors, individuals serve as trusted adults who
provide encouragement, guidance, and practical assistance, helping students
navigate the complexities of college life and early adulthood. The primary aim of mentoring is not to resolve all issues,
but rather to empower students, foster their confidence, and assist them in
developing the skills and networks necessary for long-term success. Build a Supportive
Relationship Meet
     regularly with your mentee, biweekly and for any special programing from
     ISF (quarterly). Create
     a safe and non-judgmental environment for open conversation. Utilize
     active listening and offer encouragement. Establish
     healthy boundaries while developing a trusting relationship. Provide Practical
Life Guidance Mentors are not expected to be experts in all areas, but should be resourceful and willing to connect students with available resources. ISF offers support and resources to mentors as needed. Support
     mentees in budgeting and financial literacy. Assist
     with time management and goal setting. Help
     mentees navigate housing options. Guide
     students through internship and career exploration. Promote
     professional communication skills. Facilitate
     networking opportunities and career connections. Offer
     advice on graduate school or career pathways. Support Student
Growth Help
     mentees identify their strengths and build self-confidence. Encourage
     independence and support decision-making. Provide
     guidance during challenging times, such as exams, holidays, or housing
     transitions. Encourage spiritual formation and emotional
health Engage in opportunities and discussions on
leadership development Maintain Program
Partnership Participate
     in mentor orientation or training sessions. Communicate
     with program staff if concerns arise. Refer
     students to program resources or professional services when necessary. Attend
     the annual leadership retreat (optional). Attend
     the annual mission trip if applicable. Mentor Qualities Successful mentors demonstrate the following qualities: Empathy
     and trauma-informed awareness Reliability
     and consistency Respect
     for diverse backgrounds and experiences Strong
     listening and communication skills Encouragement
     of student autonomy and growth Vibrant commitment to personal and spiritual
     growth Mentors recognize that trust is established through
patience, consistency, and respect. Preferred
Backgrounds (Not Required) Mentors may come from a variety of professional or personal
backgrounds, including: Professionals
     with career experience or networks Individuals
     who were first-generation college students Adults
     with experience in education, counseling, or youth development Individuals
     with lived experience overcoming adversity or foster care The most important qualification is a genuine commitment to
supporting a student’s success. Share this volunteer opportunity!

description_short: SMART Elections is building the most comprehensive, nonpartisan, data-based Election Verification Program in U.S. history, and we need your help! Our Online Spring Training Sessions help you learn…
description_long: SMART Elections is building the most comprehensive, nonpartisan, data-based Election Verification Program in U.S. history, and we need your help! Our Online Spring Training Sessions help you learn about observation and verification of each step of the election process. All sessions take place at 4pm PT / 7pm ET Session Topics: Tues April 21: In-person Voting Observation (Early Voting & Election Day) + Bonus: Poll Tapes! Tues April 28: Logic & Accuracy Observation Tues May 5:   Voter Rolls Tues May 12: Mail-in Processing & Dropboxes Tues May 19: Observing Post-Election Audits Tues May 26: Election Night Reporting Each training session is one hour long.  After training, we'll help you get registered at the local level to observe your election. Your time commitment can be as little as a few hours during the primary and general election in your city. Join us TODAY by signing up here or visit our website to read more about our movement at www.smartelections.us Share this volunteer opportunity!

description_short: Please only sign up for 1 shift. Our Volunteer Team will then contact you to help get you officially scheduled. Please do not come to the shelter until you have been contacted & scheduled. The SPCA…
description_long: Please only sign up for 1 shift. Our Volunteer Team will then contact you to help get you officially scheduled. Please do not come to the shelter until you have been contacted & scheduled. The SPCA of Texas is currently seeking volunteers to assist with shelter enrichment. Shelter enrichment means providing animals under managed care (shelter) with the environmental stimuli they need to remain physically and emotionally healthy. Any novel stimulus which evokes an animal’s interest can be considered enriching, including natural and artificial objects, scents, novel foods and different methods of preparing foods, and new activities of all kinds. Enrichment will take place daily. This will include preparing the novelty treats, distributing them to dogs and cats, monitoring the reactions of each animal, and any clean-up that might need to occur as a result of the treat. We provide flexible shifts every day of the week, from 9 am to 6 pm. Volunteers aged 10 and above are welcome to participate. For volunteers under 15, involvement alongside a parent or guardian aged 18 or older is required. Younger volunteers, aged 10 to 11, will primarily focus on cat handling after training, while those aged 12 and older can handle both dogs and cats. To begin volunteering, you'll need to create a volunteer account and complete a few initial steps. There is a $15 volunteer fee per person to cover the initial costs of onboarding new volunteers. Once these steps are completed, you'll be eligible to sign up for an Orientation session, where you'll receive further training and information. The process to become a volunteer is as follows: Express your interest through Voly. You will then be contacted with a link to complete a SPCA of Texas volunteer application. Upon completing the application, you will need to fulfill a few initial steps. After your initial steps are completed, you should have access through your volunteer account to see and sign up for Orientations. Please refrain from going to the shelter until your scheduled Orientation date. Share this volunteer opportunity!

description_short: Please only sign up for one shift. Our Volunteer Team will then contact you to help you get officially scheduled. Please do not go to PetSmart until we have contacted you. We deeply appreciate your…
description_long:
Please only sign up for one shift. Our Volunteer Team will then contact you to help you get officially scheduled. Please do not go to PetSmart until we have contacted you. We deeply appreciate your interest in volunteering at one of our PetSmart locations. Volunteers play a crucial role in our efforts to care for, socialize, and facilitate adoptions for our cats. Here are the key details about volunteering: Age Requirements: Volunteers must be 18 years or older to volunteer independently. For individuals aged 10-17, volunteering is permitted with a parent or guardian who is at least 18 years old. Time Commitment: This opportunity involves a regular weekly shift lasting 2 hours and requires a long-term commitment of at least 2 months. Adoption Volunteer Shifts: Volunteers assist in finding forever homes for cats during these shifts, which are available at various times throughout each day. Cleaning Volunteer Shifts: Volunteers assist with cleaning kennels, providing enrichment, and socializing with the cats. Shifts are available Mondays through Saturdays, with AM shifts from 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM and PM shifts from 6:00 PM to 9:00 PM. Sunday shifts vary due to store opening and closing times. Our locations include: City Place (75204)
Greenville Ave. (75206)
Lakewood (75214)
Denton (76205) Whether you can volunteer once a week or more frequently, the choice is entirely yours and depends on your availability and commitment. Your dedication is invaluable to us! The process to become a volunteer is as follows: Express your interest through Voly. You will then be contacted with a link to complete a SPCA of Texas volunteer application. Upon completing the application, you will need to fulfill a few initial steps. A SPCA of Texas staff person will contact you. Please refrain from going to PetSmart until we have scheduled you to get started. Share this volunteer opportunity!

CALLING ALL PRAYER WARRIORS: Join the world wide SoupMobile
Prayer Team as a Prayer Warrior and help us pray for the sick, the homeless,
the hurting & others in need. We currently have more than…
description_long:
CALLING ALL PRAYER WARRIORS: Join the world wide SoupMobile
Prayer Team as a Prayer Warrior and help us pray for the sick, the homeless,
the hurting & others in need. We currently have more than 7,000+ SoupMobile
Prayer Warriors world wide including all 50 States and a host of countries
including Europe, Australia, Africa, Ukraine, Mexico, Dubai, Canada, South
America and many more. Every week we will email you the prayer requests we have
received from all over the world, and you pray over them in the privacy of your
own home. If you believe in the power of prayer and seek to help your
fellowman, then this volunteer opportunity is just for you! This is a wonderful
'VIRTUAL' volunteer opportunity whereby from the privacy of your own home and
thru prayer, you can make a real difference in the lives of the less fortunate!
Because this is a 'virtual' volunteer opportunity, it doesn't matter if you live
in Dallas, New York, San Francisco, Japan, Zimbabwe, Germany, Mexico, Canada,
Australia or anywhere else in the world. As long as you have a valid email
address, you are good to go! May the Lord bless & keep you. Pastor David
Timothy-SoupMobile Church 2423 S. Good Latimer Expy. Dallas,Texas 75215 Disclaimer: Please understand that because this is a 'virtual' volunteer
opportunity, the SoupMobile is NOT authorized to sign off on volunteer hours.
If you join the SoupMobile Prayer Team, it will have to be because your heart
leads you to do so, not because of the service hours. Share this volunteer opportunity!

Your projects will include: Sending personalized outreach emails to prospective signers identified by the research team Following up persistently and professionally with non-responders Answering questions from prospective signers and guiding them through the online signing process Tracking outreach activity and reporting conversion rates to PTP leadership Learn more about this volunteer role here: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1UhGFn2icIEyYG4aG9GFdnNYxpMxHLnq3KLE5boemUI4/edit?usp=sharing Apply for this position here: https://www.protruthpledge.org/volunteer/ Share this volunteer opportunity!

Your projects will include: Searching public directories, LinkedIn, and online sources to find and verify contact details Organizing prospect data into structured spreadsheets and checking for accuracy and duplicates Prioritizing targets based on public visibility and influence Handing off research packages to the Outreach Liaison team on a regular cadence Learn more about this role here: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1xenGc-1hC4xO0_H8Pvzz-WKutswhQBEivChP0PHdxc8/edit?usp=sharing Apply for this volunteer position here: https://www.protruthpledge.org/volunteer/ Share this volunteer opportunity!

description_short: Refresh NTX is changing lives by providing free hygiene products to 2,500 children across 11 North Texas communities. For children living in poverty, something as simple as a toothbrush, deodorant,…
description_long: Refresh NTX is changing lives by providing free hygiene products to 2,500 children across 11 North Texas communities. For children living in poverty, something as simple as a toothbrush, deodorant, or shampoo can mean the difference between confidence and humiliation. Hygiene insecurity often leads to bullying, missed school, and lasting emotional challenges—but together, we can change that. This year's gala promises to be our biggest and most exciting yet—and we're almost sold out! Guests will enjoy an elegant evening of dinner, casino games, live entertainment, and an inspiring keynote conversation featuring three-time Super Bowl Champion and Dallas Cowboys legend Darren Woodson, interviewed by Texas Radio Hall of Fame broadcaster Norm Hitzges. Darren's remarkable story of overcoming childhood poverty perfectly reflects the hope and resilience at the heart of Refresh NTX. Because Refresh NTX is powered entirely by volunteers, every helping hand makes a real difference. With no paid staff, approximately 95% of gala proceeds go directly toward providing hygiene products for children who need them most. Whether you love decorating, greeting guests, serving dinner, helping with the wine or whiskey pull, assisting at the Butt Sketch station, or working behind the scenes, there's a volunteer role for you. You'll meet incredible people, be part of an unforgettable evening, and know your time is creating brighter futures for thousands of local children. Watch the 30-second video below to hear Darren Woodson share why he believes in Refresh NTX. https://youtube.com/shorts/tEZ73WDVboQ?si=DCYO_DA12TSlxcrv Most importantly, join us in making sure we never have to say "no" to a child who needs our help. We'd love to have you on our team! Share this volunteer opportunity!

description_long: Are you passionate about social media, design, or storytelling? For Love & Art is forming a volunteer Social Media Content Creation Team to help bring the Art Experience to people with limited mobility and improve their quality of life through therapeutic art. Volunteers will meet (primarily on Saturday mornings) to plan and create content that showcases our programs, celebrates our seniors, and inspires the community to get involved. You can join as an individual or help build a small creative team around one of the roles below. This is a great opportunity for students, early‑career professionals, or anyone wanting to build a social media portfolio while supporting a local nonprofit mission.

opportunity_title: Retired Teachers Know How To Impact Lives. Serve as a Virtual Mentor!
description_short:
Mentors play a crucial role in supporting college and trade
school students who have aged out of the foster care system. Many of these
students face challenges such as instability, limited family…
description_long:
Mentors play a crucial role in supporting college and trade
school students who have aged out of the foster care system. Many of these
students face challenges such as instability, limited family support, and gaps
in life-skills training. As mentors, individuals serve as trusted adults who
provide encouragement, guidance, and practical assistance, helping students
navigate the complexities of college life and early adulthood. The primary aim of mentoring is not to resolve all issues,
but rather to empower students, foster their confidence, and assist them in
developing the skills and networks necessary for long-term success. Build a Supportive
Relationship Meet
     regularly with your mentee, biweekly and for any special programing from
     ISF (quarterly). Create
     a safe and non-judgmental environment for open conversation. Utilize
     active listening and offer encouragement. Establish
     healthy boundaries while developing a trusting relationship. Provide Practical
Life Guidance Mentors are not expected to be experts in all areas, but should be resourceful and willing to connect students with available resources. ISF offers support and resources to mentors as needed. Support
     mentees in budgeting and financial literacy. Assist
     with time management and goal setting. Help
     mentees navigate housing options. Guide
     students through internship and career exploration. Promote
     professional communication skills. Facilitate
     networking opportunities and career connections. Offer
     advice on graduate school or career pathways. Support Student
Growth Help
     mentees identify their strengths and build self-confidence. Encourage
     independence and support decision-making. Provide
     guidance during challenging times, such as exams, holidays, or housing
     transitions. Encourage spiritual formation and emotional
health Engage in opportunities and discussions on
leadership development Maintain Program
Partnership Participate
     in mentor orientation or training sessions. Communicate
     with program staff if concerns arise. Refer
     students to program resources or professional services when necessary. Attend
     the annual leadership retreat (optional). Attend
     the annual mission trip if applicable. Mentor Qualities Successful mentors demonstrate the following qualities: Empathy
     and trauma-informed awareness Reliability
     and consistency Respect

description_long: Lleve su actitud positiva y preocupación por los demás a quienes enfrentan desafíos al final de la vida. Ilumine su día con visitas regulares, escuche sus historias, lea, juegue, observe, ayude con las actividades, ayude a la familia. Trabaje directamente con pacientes y familias en su propia comunidad como un oyente comprometido que brinda compañía y apoyo, traducción y/o alivio para el cuidador. Puede ofrecerse como voluntario los días de semana, las tardes, los fines de semana, etc. Por lo general, los voluntarios ven a los pacientes una vez a la semana o cada dos semanas. La necesidad principal es la compañía del paciente, simplemente estar allí para marcar una diferencia positiva en la vida de otra persona. Bring your positive attitude and concern for others to those facing end of life challenges. Brighten their day with regular visits, listen to their stories, read, play games, watch, assist with activities, assist the family. Work directly with patients and families in your own community as a committed listener providing companionship and support, translation, and/or relief for the caregiver. You may volunteer weekdays, evenings, weekends, etc. Usually, volunteers see patients once a week or every other week. The primary need is patient companionship-simply being there to make a positive difference in the life of another Share this volunteer opportunity!

description_short: Volunteers are the heart of hospice care. At Angel Hands Hospice, we believe in the power of presence. Our trained volunteers offer companionship and comfort to patients and their families during one…
description_long: Volunteers are the heart of hospice care. At Angel Hands Hospice, we believe in the power of presence. Our trained volunteers offer companionship and comfort to patients and their families during one of the most sacred transitions in life. Please note: Volunteers never provide hands-on medical or personal care such as bathing, lifting, administering medications, or wound care. Their role is to enhance quality of life through companionship. Volunteers are asked to respect each patient’s cultural and spiritual beliefs without leading faith-based interactions. Why Volunteer With Us? Hospice volunteering is a unique opportunity to make a meaningful impact in someone’s life. Whether you’re sitting quietly with a patient, reading their favorite book, or giving their caregiver a short break, your presence truly matters. We follow strict guidelines to ensure volunteers do not replace paid staff but instead provide enrichment and companionship that complements clinical care. Volunteer Roles We offer a variety of meaningful ways to serve, tailored to your interests and talents: Patient Companion Volunteer Sit bedside and offer quiet presence Read books or poetry aloud Play calming music or simply listen Paint a patient’s nails or style their hair gently (with permission) Watch shows Legacy Project Volunteer Help patients create a Legacy eBook by assisting in answering questions, helping to find the right photos, and digitally creating the eBook Transcribe life reflections into a beautiful digital keepsake for families Assist with journaling, letter writing, or memory boxes Put together the Legacy E-Book Administrative & Office Support Assist with data entry, mailings, and organizing supplies Assemble comfort kits or new volunteer welcome packets Create birthday, Valentines Day, and other special holiday or anniversary cards Create crafts that patients and families can use as keepsakes Creative & Specialized Roles Share skills like music, art, or guided meditation Offer pet therapy (with appropriate certification) Support memorial events or bereavement card writing Contribute photography, graphic design, or writing to our volunteer newsletter Is Hospice Volunteering Right for Me? You don’t need medical experience or special training to serve, just a heart for presence. You might thrive as a hospice volunteer if you: Are comfortable with quiet, reflective moments Want to support patients and families with dignity and empathy Can listen without needing to fix, change, or advise Respect all beliefs and traditions Feel called to honor life’s final chapter through service If this sounds like you, we’d be honored to walk this journey with you. Volunteer Training & Requirements We take our training seriously to ensure safety, compassion, and professionalism. Our training program is different than you’ll see in other hospice and palliative programs because it was created by a death doula, making it doula-led and doula-informed. Our training program has all of the information that we are required to teach you, but it also has additional information that we hope will bring an extra level of compassion and empathy to our patients, while also giving you, the volunteer, the potential to learn new skills. All volunteers must: Complete a free orientation and training Pass a background check Be at least 16 years old with a drivers license Commit to respectful interaction with patients including HIPAA Training Includes: Hospice philosophy and patient-centered care Active listening, empathy, and grief awareness Boundaries, cultural sensitivity, and ethics HIPAA confidentiality and infection control Volunteer role descriptions and expectations And more Share this volunteer opportunity!

description_short: Volunteers are the heart of hospice care. At Angel Hands Hospice, we believe in the power of presence. Our trained volunteers offer companionship and comfort to patients and their families during one…
description_long: Volunteers are the heart of hospice care. At Angel Hands Hospice, we believe in the power of presence. Our trained volunteers offer companionship and comfort to patients and their families during one of the most sacred transitions in life. Please note: Volunteers never provide hands-on medical or personal care such as bathing, lifting, administering medications, or wound care. Their role is to enhance quality of life through companionship. Volunteers are asked to respect each patient’s cultural and spiritual beliefs without leading faith-based interactions. Why Volunteer With Us? Hospice volunteering is a unique opportunity to make a meaningful impact in someone’s life. Whether you’re sitting quietly with a patient, reading their favorite book, or giving their caregiver a short break, your presence truly matters. We follow strict guidelines to ensure volunteers do not replace paid staff but instead provide enrichment and companionship that complements clinical care. Volunteer Roles We offer a variety of meaningful ways to serve, tailored to your interests and talents: Patient Companion Volunteer Sit bedside and offer quiet presence Read books or poetry aloud Play calming music or simply listen Paint a patient’s nails or style their hair gently (with permission) Watch shows Legacy Project Volunteer Help patients create a Legacy eBook by assisting in answering questions, helping to find the right photos, and digitally creating the eBook Transcribe life reflections into a beautiful digital keepsake for families Assist with journaling, letter writing, or memory boxes Put together the Legacy E-Book Administrative & Office Support Assist with data entry, mailings, and organizing supplies Assemble comfort kits or new volunteer welcome packets Create birthday, Valentines Day, and other special holiday or anniversary cards Create crafts that patients and families can use as keepsakes Creative & Specialized Roles Share skills like music, art, or guided meditation Offer pet therapy (with appropriate certification) Support memorial events or bereavement card writing Contribute photography, graphic design, or writing to our volunteer newsletter Is Hospice Volunteering Right for Me? You don’t need medical experience or special training to serve, just a heart for presence. You might thrive as a hospice volunteer if you: Are comfortable with quiet, reflective moments Want to support patients and families with dignity and empathy Can listen without needing to fix, change, or advise Respect all beliefs and traditions Feel called to honor life’s final chapter through service If this sounds like you, we’d be honored to walk this journey with you. Volunteer Training & Requirements We take our training seriously to ensure safety, compassion, and professionalism. Our training program is different than you’ll see in other hospice and palliative programs because it was created by a death doula, making it doula-led and doula-informed. Our training program has all of the information that we are required to teach you, but it also has additional information that we hope will bring an extra level of compassion and empathy to our patients, while also giving you, the volunteer, the potential to learn new skills. All volunteers must: Complete a free orientation and training Pass a background check Be at least 16 years old with a drivers license Commit to respectful interaction with patients including HIPAA Training Includes: Hospice philosophy and patient-centered care Active listening, empathy, and grief awareness Boundaries, cultural sensitivity, and ethics HIPAA confidentiality and infection control Volunteer role descriptions and expectations And more Share this volunteer opportunity!
